prevalence.trend: stable; higher in women and older adults

incidence.source: Primary care and GI cohort syntheses

burden.dalyPerCapita: low mortality; significant QOL and productivity impact

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Rome IV functional constipation criteria; exclude secondary causes and alarm features

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate; improves with diet and laxative adherence

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Clinical diagnosis in primary care; limited access to motility testing
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Dietary fiber and fluids, osmotic laxatives (PEG/lactulose), stimulant laxatives short-term; address secondary causes
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Low fiber diets and inconsistent long-term follow-up
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Low-cost laxatives and primary care access in many settings
